mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut periods over a 5-year walk-forward test, or Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market slack signals deteriorating consumer spending power and corporate earnings pressure, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital goods demand, which compounds corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects immediate supply-side capacity utilization and inventory replenishment cycles, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during periods of stable monetary policy.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of INDPRO acceleration episodes, or the signal reverses direction during rate-hike cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 210.0 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 210.0, or the signal fails to predict direction with >55% accuracy over a 4-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad-based economic expansion that lifts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of GDP growth periods, or the Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all rolling 3-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 95.0 signals severe manufacturing contraction and supply chain bottlenecks, which depress corporate profit margins and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where INDPRO drops below 95.0, or the signal predictive accuracy falls below 50% in walk-forward testing.
